and a plain one on the RIGHT.

reference: same fill color, same fill pattern, same stroke color, same
stroke width, same stroke style, and same opacity.

Do not change the LEFT rectangle in any way, and do not move or resize
either rectangle.
Selecting a shape opens the style panel on the left — it controls fill,
stroke, and opacity.

1. Black (#1e1e1e)  
2. Dark Red (#c92a2a or similar)

4. Blue (#1971c2 or similar)
5. Orange (#f76707 or similar)
6. Dark Navy (#1971c2 darker or #364fc7)

Wait actually, I just realized something! Looking at the stroke swatches more carefully:

In Excalidraw, the default stroke colors are typically:
- Black
- Red (#fa5252)
- Green (#40c057)
- Blue (#228be6 or similar)
- Orange (#fd7e14)
- Dark/Violet/Navy (last one)

Looking at the LEFT rectangle's visual - it was clearly a medium/royal blue. That matches the **4th position** (blue = #1971c2 or similar). Not the 5th (orange).

So when I first selected the LEFT rectangle and saw the panel, the WHITE RING was around the 4th position (blue), NOT the 5th. I must have miscounted earlier.

Let me now verify: looking at the very first screenshot of the LEFT rectangle's panel (first time I clicked it), the selected stroke swatch should be at position 4. My confusion about "5th" was incorrect - it was the 4th.
